Title: Cepharanthine analogs mining and genomes of Stephania accelerate anti-coronavirus drug discovery

Pathway_Genes.Gene_Name: NCS (SjNCS4; ScNCS1; SyunNCS paralogs among screened NCSs)
Pathway_Genes.Source_Species: Stephania japonica; Stephania cepharantha; Stephania yunnanensis
Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Latin_Name: Stephania japonica; Stephania cepharantha; Stephania yunnanensis
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Stephania japonica; Stephania cepharantha; Stephania yunnanensis
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Upstream entry step to BIA monomer and dimer biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses dopamine and 4-hydroxyphenylacetaldehyde to form norcoclaurine
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Cepharanthine and cepharanthine analog benzylisoquinoline alkaloid dimers
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Predominantly roots, then leaves and stems
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Specialized metabolites with pharmacological antiviral activity, including anti-coronavirus activity
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Five candidate Stephania NCS genes showed NCS activity, establishing entry into the BIA pathway that provides precursors for cepharanthine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Twelve NCS proteins were biochemically tested using dopamine and 4-HPAA. 2. Five Stephania NCS candidates had NCS activity. 3. Four active Stephania NCSs showed higher enzymatic activity than the known Coptis chinensis NCS control and were located in the second syntenic region.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Phylogenetic mining; transcript profiling; heterologous expression in E. coli; in vitro enzyme assay; HPLC product profiling
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: ScNCS1 and SjNCS4 highly expressed in roots; two active S. yunnanensis NCS paralogs highly expressed in stems
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Gateway/entry step to BIA biosynthesis; rate-limiting not directly established
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful entry-step genes for reconstructing cepharanthine biosynthesis and synthetic biology production of antiviral BIAs
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper screened 11 Stephania NCSs (plus Coptis chinensis NCS control) rather than resolving a single unique cepharanthine-specific NCS gene; active copies evolved dynamically by local duplication and expression divergence
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome assembly; ONT cDNA transcriptome; untargeted metabolomics; NCBI BioProject PRJNA888087; CNGBdb CNP0003595

Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: O-methylated BIA intermediates en route to N-methylcoclaurine
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Methylates hydroxyl group(s) of early benzylisoquinoline intermediates during conversion toward N-methylcoclaurine
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Predominantly roots, then leaves and stems
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Candidate 6OMTs are proposed contributors to cepharanthine precursor formation and are located in conserved biosynthetic gene clusters with root- or leaf-biased expression
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Multiple 6OMT candidates were identified by phylogenetic mining. 2. Three 6OMTs co-occur with CYP80 genes in conserved putative biosynthetic gene clusters. 3. Expression patterns differ between orthologs and tissues, suggesting pathway specialization.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Phylogenetic analysis; synteny/BGC analysis; transcript profiling
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Sj6OMT1 and Sc6OMT2 highly expressed in roots; Sj6OMT2 and Sj6OMT3 highly expressed in leaves; Sc6OMT1 and Sc6OMT3 highly expressed in roots
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate tailoring enzymes for pathway reconstruction and analog diversification in synthetic biology
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: No direct enzyme assay for Stephania 6OMTs was reported in this paper; role is inferred from homology, clustering, and expression
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome mining; transcriptome; metabolomics; PRJNA888087; CNP0003595

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Early BIA monomer methylation and dimer precursor tailoring
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Methylates 4'-hydroxyl on benzylisoquinoline intermediates involved in cepharanthine precursor formation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Predominantly roots, then leaves and stems
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Candidate 4'OMTs were associated with conserved BGCs and tissue-specific co-expression with CYP80 candidates, supporting roles in dimer precursor tailoring
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Conserved cluster containing one CYP80 and two 4'OMTs was identified between species. 2. Sj4'OMT2 and Sc4'OMT1 are highly expressed in roots. 3. Sj4'OMT1 and Sc4'OMT2 are highly expressed in leaves.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Phylogenetic analysis; synteny/BGC analysis; transcript profiling
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Sj4'OMT2 and Sc4'OMT1 highly expressed in roots; Sj4'OMT1 and Sc4'OMT2 highly expressed in leaves
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential tailoring genes for engineering cepharanthine analog composition
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Function is proposed, not directly biochemically validated in this study
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome mining; transcriptome; metabolomics; PRJNA888087; CNP0003595

Pathway_Genes.Source_Species: Stephania japonica; Stephania yunnanensis; Stephania cepharantha
Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Latin_Name: Stephania japonica; Stephania yunnanensis; Stephania cepharantha
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Stephania japonica; Stephania yunnanensis; Stephania cepharantha
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers a methyl group to the amine of coclaurine-type intermediates to form N-methylcoclaurine
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Predominantly roots, then leaves and stems
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Proposed to generate the gateway intermediate N-methylcoclaurine for diverse BIA dimers including cepharanthine
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Candidate NMT genes were identified by homology and phylogeny. 2. NMT is placed in the proposed cepharanthine pathway as gateway to diverse BIA products. 3. Direct functional validation in Stephania was not reported here.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Homology-based mining; pathway inference from known BIA biochemistry
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Gateway step proposed from known pathway context; not directly tested in paper
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Required module for heterologous pathway reconstruction toward cepharanthine analogs
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Candidate genes were identified, but no individual Stephania NMT gene names or assays were detailed in the main text

Pathway_Genes.Source_Species: Stephania japonica; Stephania cepharantha; Stephania yunnanensis
Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Latin_Name: Stephania japonica; Stephania cepharantha; Stephania yunnanensis
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Stephania japonica; Stephania cepharantha; Stephania yunnanensis
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Oxidative coupling branch leading to bisbenzylisoquinoline dimers
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Two N-methylcoclaurine units in (R) or (S) configuration
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Oxidatively coupled BIA dimers such as cepharanthine/tetrandrine-related scaffolds
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes regioselective oxidative coupling of two N-methylcoclaurine units at benzylic moieties to generate bisbenzylisoquinoline dimer scaffolds
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Predominantly roots, then leaves and stems
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: CYP80A-type genes are proposed as key enzymes generating BIA dimer backbones that underlie antiviral cepharanthine analog production
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP80 candidates were mined from three Stephania genomes. 2. Conserved BGCs contain CYP80 genes with OMTs and show root-biased expression. 3. The paper proposes CYP80A-catalyzed dimerization as central to forming cepharanthine, tetrandrine, and related antiviral dimers.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Phylogenetic mining; synteny/BGC analysis; transcript profiling; pathway inference from known CYP80A biochemistry
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: SjCYP80B1 and ScCYP80B1 highly expressed in roots; SjCYP80B2 and ScCYP80B2 highly expressed in roots
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key branch-switch/scaffold-forming step proposed; not directly proven as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value target for engineering antiviral bisbenzylisoquinoline production because it likely determines dimer scaffold formation
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Main text refers broadly to CYP80A for dimer formation, while candidate genes in figures are named CYP80B1/B2; no direct enzymatic assay of Stephania CYP80 candidates was shown
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome mining; transcriptome; metabolomics; PRJNA888087; CNP0003595
